title: Isoglossa collina
taxonomy: Isoglossa collina is classified within the family Acanthaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus or thorny flowers. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, including Acanthoideae, Nelsonioideae, and Thunbergioideae. Isoglossa collina belongs to the tribe Justicieae, which is characterized by its unique floral morphology and arrangement. The species is also classified within the genus Isoglossa, which includes several other species of flowering plants native to tropical regions. Isoglossa collina's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the family Acanthaceae.

intro: Isoglossa collina is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, native to tropical regions of Africa. Traditionally, Isoglossa collina has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, due to its reported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Isoglossa collina include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
